steps.title: Set up the area equation
steps.content:
Let $h$ be the height. The base is $h+10$, and the triangle area is $208$.
$$208=\frac{1}{2}h(h+10)$$

steps.title: Test the likely height
steps.content: The answer choices are integers, so test the height that makes the area work. $$\frac{1}{2}(16)(16+10)=\frac{1}{2}(16)(26)=208$$ A height of $16$ centimeters gives the required area. The answer is B.
